metadata.title: Zekelman Standard 3 Source Verification Tracking
metadata.purpose: Human source-verification and final teacher-decision support for unresolved Standard 3 strands.
metadata.canonical_source_policy: Zekelman 2025 remains canonical for wording, numbering, level progression, and scope.
metadata.supplemental_source_policy: Loshon HaTorah / Loshon Hakodesh evidence is supplemental only and does not create teacher decisions.
metadata.runtime_policy: No item in this tracking file is runtime-ready or question-ready.

primary_verification_items.canonical_zekelman_expectation_summary: Students group a פסוק into meaningful phrases and adjust Hebrew word order when translating into English, moving from learned פסוקים to unlearned פסוקים with increasing independence.

current_draft_skill_mappings.skill_label: Group phrases and adjust Hebrew word order for translation

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 4 / printed p. 6, Lesson 1
source_references.evidence_summary: Explains that Torah wording can place the verb before or after the noun.
source_references.review_caution: Verify the Hebrew examples and printed page directly in the PDF.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 4 / printed p. 6, Lesson 1
source_references.evidence_summary: Explains translating literal Hebrew word order into natural English order.
source_references.review_caution: Strong support for reordering, but not automatic approval for compact question formats.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 6 / printed p. 10, Lesson 2
source_references.evidence_summary: Explains that two nouns side by side can convey 'the __ of __'.
source_references.review_caution: Decide whether this supports 3.08 or should remain primarily under 3.04.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 80 / printed p. 158, Lesson 16
source_references.evidence_summary: Missing-word discussion around בראשית illustrates why construct-like phrase translation can require context.
source_references.review_caution: Use as a caution boundary, not as an active question pattern.

primary_verification_items.evidence_strength_notes: Strong for word-order and English reordering; partial for the full 3.08 phrase-grouping scope.
what_reviewer_must_verify: Whether Loshon word-order evidence is close enough to Zekelman 3.08 wording to support a distinct diagnostic planning lane.
Whether grouping and word-order work must remain full-context only.
Whether סמיכות support belongs under 3.04, 3.08, or both.
Whether extracted page references are reliable enough given partial OCR.
specific_reviewer_questions: Is variable verb/noun order sufficient to treat 3.08 as a distinct reviewable diagnostic direction?
Should natural English reordering be approved only for full-pasuk or phrase-translation review?
Should סמיכות-based grouping stay under 3.04 until a later phase?
Does the answer booklet clarify the main-book rule without expanding scope beyond Zekelman?
unresolved_hebrew_ocr_table_concerns: Main-book extraction is partial and needs direct PDF inspection.
Hebrew examples and printed page references need visual verification.
Answer-booklet evidence must not become an active answer key.
primary_verification_items.reviewer_notes: Approve as a diagnostic planning direction only when narrowed to phrase-level translation and basic Hebrew-to-English word-order adjustment. Do not yet use as a broad independent full-pasuk grouping skill. סמיכות may support this lane, but should remain separately tracked under 3.04 as well.
primary_verification_items.remaining_source_checks: Verify Loshon page references and examples visually in the PDFs.
primary_verification_items.recommended_next_action: Create a narrowed 3.08 diagnostic planning lane after source verification.

primary_verification_items.strand_name: Parts of Speech / Nouns and Adjectives
primary_verification_items.canonical_zekelman_expectation_summary: Students move from identifying nouns and adjectives in learned words to recognizing gender, number, irregular forms, and סמיכות in unlearned פסוקים.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary
source_references.evidence_summary: Summary covers noun gender markers and common plural endings with exceptions.
source_references.review_caution: Summary is compressed; verify original rule pages and exceptions directly.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 6 / printed p. 10, Lesson 2
source_references.evidence_summary: Explains construct relationship through two nouns side by side.
source_references.review_caution: Decide whether סמיכות should receive a separate decision from noun/adjective features.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 6 and page 81 summary
source_references.evidence_summary: Supports common construct-form changes such as final ה or ים shifts.
source_references.review_caution: Do not generalize beyond source-verified forms.

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary
source_references.evidence_summary: Flags exception-like noun forms and English number mismatch.
source_references.review_caution: Likely a caution note rather than a foundational diagnostic lane.

primary_verification_items.evidence_strength_notes: Strong for noun features and סמיכות examples; partial for full adjective and level-progression decisions.
what_reviewer_must_verify: Whether noun/adjective features and סמיכות should receive separate final decisions.
Whether סמיכות is suitable for standalone diagnostic planning.
Whether Loshon noun-feature evidence aligns with Zekelman 2025 level progression.
Whether irregular noun forms and adjective agreement belong in the current review phase.
specific_reviewer_questions: Should std3_04_noun_features and std3_04_smichut_recognition receive separate decisions?
Does Loshon support enough of סמיכות to help review 3.08?
Are plural-looking singular English forms suitable for diagnostic planning or only caution notes?
How should OCR-sensitive Hebrew examples be verified before later blueprint use?
unresolved_hebrew_ocr_table_concerns: סמיכות examples require direct PDF verification of forms and nekudos.
Main-book summary page is compressed and should be checked against original rule pages.
Answer-booklet examples are review evidence only, not active answer-key material.
primary_verification_items.reviewer_notes: Approve with wording revision. Split into at least two planning lanes: noun/adjective features and סמיכות recognition. Noun gender, number, and common form changes are suitable for diagnostic planning after source examples are verified. Irregular forms and adjective agreement should remain later-level or review-required.
primary_verification_items.remaining_source_checks: Verify Hebrew examples, nekudos, and level placement against Zekelman and Loshon PDFs.
primary_verification_items.recommended_next_action: Add separate planning lanes for noun features and סמיכות.

primary_verification_items.canonical_zekelman_expectation_summary: Advanced students explain systems of תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and the interaction between ניקוד and טעמי המקרא. Lower levels show no clearly scoped expectation in the current extraction.

current_draft_skill_mappings.skill_label: Recognize advanced ניקוד patterns and syllable rules

source_references.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary, citing pg. 9
source_references.review_caution: Narrow evidence only; not enough to support full 3.10 scope.

source_references.page_or_section: answer extracted pages 22 and 29, citing main-book p. 96
source_references.evidence_summary: Mentions pausal form or end-of-sentence vowel changes.
source_references.review_caution: Main-book p. 96 must be checked directly; answer booklet is supplemental only.

primary_verification_items.evidence_strength_notes: Evidence flags nikud-sensitive issues but does not cover the full Zekelman advanced ניקוד standard.
what_reviewer_must_verify: Whether 3.10 should remain deferred pending direct canonical and Loshon PDF review.
Whether any small nikud-sensitive subskill should be reviewed with 3.06.
Whether answer-booklet pausal/vowel notes are enough to guide review questions.
Whether OCR and nekudos are reliable enough to cite without direct visual review.
specific_reviewer_questions: Are levels 1-4 intentionally out of scope for 3.10?
Does limited את-family nikud evidence belong under 3.06 only, 3.10 only, or both?
Should pausal-form evidence be considered source support for 3.10 or only a later-review signal?
Which parts of תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and טעמי המקרא are actually present in the canonical Zekelman source at each level?
unresolved_hebrew_ocr_table_concerns: Nekudos and table relationships are high-risk for OCR error.
Main-book p. 96 is referenced through answer-booklet notes and needs direct PDF review.
Current evidence should not be treated as approval for 3.10.
primary_verification_items.reviewer_notes: Defer to later phase. Current evidence is too narrow and OCR-sensitive to support 3.10 as a diagnostic lane. Limited nikud-sensitive examples may be used as caution notes under 3.06, but full ניקוד systems such as תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and טעמי המקרא require direct source review before diagnostic planning.
primary_verification_items.remaining_source_checks: Directly verify Zekelman 3.10 levels and Loshon nikud/pausal references in the PDFs.
primary_verification_items.recommended_next_action: Keep 3.10 out of MVP diagnostic blueprint except as a caution note.

secondary_verification_items.what_was_already_documented: Teacher review supports foundational tense, person, and form clues as a planning lane. Advanced verb-form areas remain separate later lanes.
loshon_evidence_strengthens: present tense and command forms as later-lane evidence
secondary_verification_items.revisit_reason: New Loshon evidence may require clearer wording around deferred weak-letter, ו׳ ההיפוך, present-tense, and command-form sublanes.

secondary_verification_items.what_was_already_documented: Teacher review supports visible prefix and article identification only; the two functions of את remain deferred.
secondary_verification_items.revisit_reason: Reviewer may need to clarify whether את-family nikud evidence belongs under 3.06, 3.10, or both.

secondary_verification_items.what_was_already_documented: Teacher review requires separate lanes for pronoun referent tracking and pronominal suffix decoding.
loshon_evidence_strengthens: possessive suffix decoding on singular noun forms
possessive suffix decoding on plural noun forms
what_still_needs_caution: bundling suffix decoding with referent tracking
secondary_verification_items.revisit_reason: Reviewer may want to explicitly separate suffix decoding approval language from referent-tracking approval language.

notes: Explicit final human reviewer decisions for 3.08, 3.04, and 3.10 were provided and recorded exactly for review documentation only. No runtime-ready or question-ready status was added.
